# Jamari Anthony Jacquet — Graduation Campaign Kit
### Monarch High School, Class of 2026 · Coconut Creek / Ft. Lauderdale, FL
### "From Coconut Creek to the Codebase" 🎓💻

> Everything in this doc is copy-paste ready. Every link uses the token `{{SITE_URL}}` — swap it for the live celebration site URL before posting.

---

## 1. Campaign Overview

**Theme:** *Compiled & Committed* — a graduate who didn't just finish high school, he shipped v1.0 of himself. The whole campaign frames Jamari's graduation as a launch event: he's a future software developer, and this is his "next chapter" going live.

**Visual identity:** Monarch orange + navy. Cap-and-gown energy meets developer energy — think terminal cursors, "loading next chapter…" bars, and a confetti-meets-confetti-of-code aesthetic that lives on the 3D animated site.

**Key message (one sentence):**
> Jamari Anthony Jacquet just graduated Monarch High — and he's already building what's next.

**Primary call-to-action:** Drive everyone to the 3D animated celebration site to leave a note, see the experience, and watch his story unfold → **{{SITE_URL}}**

**Hashtag strategy:**
- **Primary branded tag:** `#Jamari2026` (use on EVERY post, every platform)
- **Campaign tag:** `#CompiledAndCommitted`
- **Supporting tags** rotate by platform from the Hashtag Bank below — keep 1 primary + 3–6 supporting on Instagram/TikTok, 1–2 on X, none-to-light on LinkedIn.

**Posting rhythm:** Tease → Reveal (grad day) → Celebrate → Drive traffic to site → Future-dev spotlight. Full 7-day plan in Section 9.

---

## 3. Instagram

### (a) Feed Post — Carousel Caption (grad photos)
> Swipe through 👉 cap, gown, and the kid who's about to change the game.

```
He did the thing. 🎓🧡💙

Jamari Anthony Jacquet — Monarch High, Class of 2026. ✅

I've watched this young man go from "let me show you something I built" to building a whole future. The cap and gown are just the screenshot — the real story is the code he's about to write.

Proud uncle doesn't even cover it. Swipe to see the kid we've all been bragging about. 👉

We built him something special to mark the moment — a whole 3D experience celebrating Jamari. Go leave him a note, you'll see why we're so proud. Link's in the bio. 🔗

#Jamari2026 #CompiledAndCommitted #ClassOf2026 #MonarchHigh #MonarchKnights #CoconutCreek #FutureSoftwareDeveloper #ProudUncle #WeMadeIt
```

### (b) Reels — Caption + Hook + On-Screen Text

**Hook (first 1.5 seconds, spoken or text):**
> "POV: your nephew just graduated AND already knows what he's building next."

**Caption:**
```
From the back of the classroom to the front of his future. 🎓➡️💻

Jamari, Monarch High Class of 2026 — and just getting started. Tap the link in bio for the full experience. 🔗🧡💙

#Jamari2026 #ClassOf2026 #grad2026 #fyp #MonarchHigh #FutureDev #CodeLife #SoFlo
```

**On-screen text beats:**
- 0:00 — `POV: your nephew just graduated 🎓`
- 0:02 — `Monarch High • Class of 2026`
- 0:05 — `Coconut Creek raised him 🌴`
- 0:08 — `…but watch what he builds next 💻`
- 0:11 — `Future software developer. Remember the name.`
- 0:14 — `Jamari Anthony Jacquet`
- 0:16 — `Link in bio → see the full experience 🔗`

### (c) Story Frames (3–4)

**Frame 1 — Announcement**
> `HE GRADUATED 🎓🧡💙`
> `Jamari • Monarch High • C/O 2026`
> *Sticker idea:* 🎉 confetti + "Class of 2026" GIF

**Frame 2 — Poll**
> `Most likely to… build the next big app? 📱`
> *Sticker idea:* Poll → "Obviously 💻" / "100% 🚀"

**Frame 3 — Countdown / Hype**
> `The whole family pulled up for this one.`
> *Sticker idea:* Emoji slider 🧡 "How proud are we?" (let it max out)

**Frame 4 — Site Drive (with link)**
> `We made him a whole 3D experience 👀`
> `Tap to celebrate Jamari 👇`
> *Sticker idea:* Link sticker → {{SITE_URL}} + "Tap here" arrow GIF

### (d) Bio-Link Blurb
```
🎓 Jamari • Monarch High C/O 2026
💻 Future software developer in the making
👇 Celebrate him here
{{SITE_URL}}
```

## 4. Facebook

### Longer Heartfelt Post (engagement-driver)
```
Some moments you wait years for. Today was one of them. 🎓🧡💙

Jamari Anthony Jacquet officially graduated from Monarch High School, Class of 2026 — and I'm not gonna lie, I had to take a second to soak it all in.

I remember when this kid would pull me over to show me something he figured out on the computer, eyes lit up, talking a hundred miles an hour. That spark never went away — it just got sharper. He's not just a graduate. He's a future software developer, and anybody who's spent five minutes around him knows he's going to build something real.

To my nephew: we are PROUD of you. Not just for the cap and gown — for the discipline, the curiosity, and the heart you put into everything. Coconut Creek raised a good one. The next chapter is yours to write (or, knowing you, to code 😄💻).

We put together something special to celebrate him — a full 3D experience where you can see his story and leave him a message. Come show him some love, he's earned every bit of it:

👉 {{SITE_URL}}

Drop a 🎓 in the comments and tell Jamari congratulations. Let's flood it. 🧡💙

#Jamari2026 #ClassOf2026 #MonarchHigh #CoconutCreek #ProudUncle #FutureSoftwareDeveloper
```

### Shorter Punchy Variant
```
My nephew Jamari just graduated Monarch High — Class of 2026! 🎓🧡💙

Future software developer, certified good kid, and the pride of this whole family. We built him a 3D celebration site — go congratulate him and leave a note 👇

{{SITE_URL}}

Drop a 🎓 below! #Jamari2026 #ClassOf2026 #MonarchHigh
```

## 6. LinkedIn (classy, door-opening)

```
A proud moment for our family — and a name worth remembering in tech.

My nephew, Jamari Anthony Jacquet, just graduated from Monarch High School in Coconut Creek, Florida (Class of 2026). What stands out about Jamari isn't only the milestone — it's the direction. He's an aspiring software developer with a genuine builder's instinct: the kind of curiosity that turns "how does this work?" into "let me build a better version."

Over the years I've watched him teach himself, iterate, and stay genuinely excited about programming. That combination — discipline plus curiosity — is exactly what the best engineers are made of, and he has it early.

To anyone in software, edtech, or early-talent mentorship: keep an eye on this one. He's the kind of emerging talent worth investing in early.

We built a small celebration experience to mark the occasion. If you'd like to leave him an encouraging word as he steps into his next chapter, it would mean a lot: {{SITE_URL}}

Congratulations, Jamari. The future is yours to build. 🎓💻

#ClassOf2026 #FutureSoftwareDeveloper #TechTalent #Jamari2026
```

## 7. TikTok

**Video concept:** A 15–30s glow-up montage. Start on baby/young photos of Jamari at a keyboard or with a controller → transition on the beat to grad day in cap and gown → final frame flips to a "terminal" overlay typing `> launching next chapter…` then the site URL. Orange + navy color grade throughout, confetti on the beat drop.

**Trending-audio suggestion:** A triumphant "glow-up / they doubted me, now look" transition sound or an uplifting orchestral-drop trend (e.g., the "started from the bottom" / "main character moment" style audios). Pick whatever is trending that day with a clear beat drop at ~0:05–0:07 for the cap-and-gown reveal.

**Caption:**
```
From little kid at the keyboard to Monarch High grad 🎓➡️💻 Class of 2026, future software developer, our whole heart. Link in bio to celebrate Jamari 🧡💙 #Jamari2026 #ClassOf2026 #grad2026 #fyp #MonarchHigh #FutureDev
```

**On-screen text beats:**
- 0:00 — `the kid who always wanted to build things…`
- 0:03 — `(always on a screen, always curious 💻)`
- 0:06 — *BEAT DROP — cap & gown reveal* → `MONARCH HIGH • CLASS OF 2026 🎓`
- 0:10 — `Coconut Creek made him. 🌴`
- 0:14 — `> launching next chapter…`
- 0:18 — `future software developer 🚀`
- 0:22 — `Jamari Anthony Jacquet`
- 0:26 — `celebrate him → link in bio 🔗`

## 8. The "Blast"

### Group Text / WhatsApp (copy-paste ready)
```
🎓🧡💙 BIG NEWS! Jamari graduated Monarch High — Class of 2026!

We're SO proud of him. The family put together a special 3D celebration site where you can see his story and leave him a message. Takes 30 seconds and would mean the world to him:

👉 {{SITE_URL}}

Please share with anyone who loves Jamari. Let's make his inbox blow up with love. 🎉
```

### Email Blast

**Subject line options (pick one):**
- `🎓 Jamari graduated! Come celebrate him (special site inside)`
- `He did it! Jamari is Monarch High, Class of 2026 🧡💙`
- `Leave Jamari a graduation message — link inside`

**Body:**
```
Hi everyone,

We've got a moment worth celebrating: Jamari Anthony Jacquet just graduated from Monarch High School, Class of 2026! 🎓🧡💙

Those of you who know Jamari know he's been all about building things and figuring out how everything works — and he's setting his sights on becoming a software developer. This graduation isn't an ending; it's a launch.

To mark the occasion, we created something special: a 3D animated celebration experience just for Jamari. You can explore it, watch his story, and — most importantly — leave him a personal message of congratulations.

Visit the celebration here:
👉 {{SITE_URL}}

It takes less than a minute, and every note he reads will mean the world to him as he steps into his next chapter.

Please feel free to forward this to family and friends who'd want to cheer him on.

With pride and a whole lot of love,
The Jacquet Family

P.S. If you share on social, tag it #Jamari2026 so we can find your post and reshare it! 🎉
```

## 9. 7-Day Posting Schedule (Eastern Time)

| Day | Platform | Content | Best Time (ET) |
|-----|----------|---------|----------------|
| Day 1 (Tease) | Instagram Story | "Big day coming…" countdown sticker + 🎓 | 6:00 PM |
| Day 1 (Tease) | X / Twitter | Hype teaser tweet ("HE DID IT 🚨" — hold link) | 8:00 PM |
| Day 2 (Reveal) | Instagram Feed | Grad photo carousel caption (3a) | 11:30 AM |
| Day 2 (Reveal) | Facebook | Longer heartfelt proud-family post (4) | 12:30 PM |
| Day 2 (Reveal) | Group Text + Email | The Blast — send to family & friends | 5:00 PM |
| Day 3 (Celebrate) | Instagram Reels | Reels montage caption (3b) | 7:00 PM |
| Day 3 (Celebrate) | X / Twitter | Heartfelt variant (5) | 9:00 AM |
| Day 4 (Drive traffic) | Instagram Story | Site-drive frame + link sticker (3d, Frame 4) | 1:00 PM |
| Day 4 (Drive traffic) | Facebook | Shorter punchy variant (4) | 6:00 PM |
| Day 5 (Future-dev) | LinkedIn | Emerging tech-talent post (6) | 9:00 AM |
| Day 5 (Future-dev) | X / Twitter | Future-dev focused variant (5) | 12:00 PM |
| Day 6 (Hype) | TikTok | Glow-up grad montage (7) | 7:30 PM |
| Day 6 (Hype) | Instagram Story | Poll frame "Most likely to build the next big app?" (3c) | 8:00 PM |
| Day 7 (Last call) | Instagram Story | "Last chance to leave Jamari a note 🔗" + link sticker | 6:00 PM |
| Day 7 (Last call) | Facebook | "Still flooding in 🧡 keep the love coming → {{SITE_URL}}" | 7:00 PM |
